title: Action-Sequence Conditioning for DiT
key_finding: MLP-flat-actseq wins on aggregate CosSim for all 3 pilot encoders (no P1 pass). Action-sequence conditioning benefits DiT more than MLP (interaction +0.008 to +0.010 at h=16), but the absolute gap remains negative. The interaction signal is modest and one MLP delta is negative (clip -0.0009), making the 2-3x ratio fragile. DiT-actseq beats copy baseline at the farthest horizon step, but this is a low bar. The interaction effect is a hypothesis-generating finding, not a demonstrated advantage.
report_framing: Negative result with modest interaction effect as hypothesis for future work
caveats: MLP-flat-actseq still wins on aggregate CosSim -- no P1 pass
MLP received same ordered action info (fair comparison)
DiT used EMA weights; MLP did not (standard practice but asymmetric)
Future actions are logged/planned, not predicted (open-loop world model framing)
